reviews.review_msg: Was worried about this prof but he turned out awesome. Does fun stuff in class and always there to help. Best Mass Com prof at CC!
Simply amazing. His passion for communication and media is infectious. Pay attention, you'll do well.
Jean would go off on a lot of tangents, that would kind of take up a lot of class time however they were generally interesting although not always helpfulrelated to what we were studying.

Professor Hebert turned out to be a really awesome teacher. Although this class has quite a lot of projects, they're reasonably sized and manageable. Jean's teaching style is pretty fun and his lectures are interactive, which is neat. I thought his grading was completely fair.

I didn't really like him. His lectures are entirely different from the stuff in the book, but his tests are from the book. Its a first year class, but he expects you to pop out answers which only a 3rd or 4th year student can. Same with his assignments. His assignments carry so little marks but they are so many in number. Its exhausting.

I did not like him as a teacher. I was interested in Comm and because of his course i will never take a class in Comm again. Terrible. I would not recommend him to anyone. He seems kind of stuck and he can seem bitter/rude/inconsiderate. He class is incomprehensible and he does a poor job explaining concepts.
He was horrible, I never disliked a professor so much.  The lectures were like watching paint dry and the final was so specific that it was just stupid.

His slides are horrible and he goes through them really quick that it's impossible to copy anything down. He does try to be a good professor and tries to make things interesting but I think the course is just boring overall. I wouldn't recommend taking his class though. He's too new and seems very disorganized and scattered.

I thought he was pretty easy. He gave you the material for the course, what was on the midterm was on the lecture notes and readings. It wasn't hard at all. TA was pretty nice too and helped out a lot.

Jean is great.  Very helpful and wants you to succeed, you just have to make the effort and ask questions if you're confused with something.  Super smart and very interested in the subject. He's like a walking library.  Don't listen to these other ratings they're horrible, he's obviously made the effort to change things. I really enjoyed his class.
